decision.hypothesis:
ESCALATE

Primary ledger and live state disagree, and this now needs ledger reconciliation rather than another repair cycle.

- The incident summary is still frozen in `state="dispatch"` with `next_expected_event="meta_repair.repair_attempt"` and `summary_text="meta-repair background-dispatched"` at [.megaplan/incident-ledger/summaries/incidents/inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/incident-ledger/summaries/incidents/inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.json:130).
- The current chain state shows the session has moved well past the m4 stall: `current_plan_name="m6a-process-runtime-core-20260704-1849"`, `last_state="between_milestones"`, `pr_number=87`, with `m4-clip-shader-keyframes` and `m5-effects-transitions-mask` both recorded `done` in [.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json:4) and [.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json:169).
- Corroboration shows the chain is live, not awaiting repair: `ps` shows the chain bash still running for `05:05:55`, tmux still has `megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e`, and the latest chain log is actively invalidating old execute sessions at [.megaplan/cloud-chain-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/cloud-chain-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log:39950).
- The last meta-repair failure is real but old: the run log ends with “codex launch failed before producing a recordable meta-repair response” at [/workspace/.megaplan/meta-runs/20260704T121718Z-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log](/workspace/.megaplan/meta-runs/20260704T121718Z-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log:7), while the latest watchdog report now classifies this session as only `status="alive"` at [/workspace/watchdog-report.json](/workspace/watchdog-report.json:113).
- I also reproduced a dirty-worktree regression in `/workspace/arnold/arnold_pipelines/megaplan/cloud/wrappers/arnold-watchdog`: the focused watchdog test incorrectly dispatched meta-repair for `failed:stopped`. Restoring that hunk to the already-correct `HEAD` behavior made the focused cloud tests pass, so no new Arnold source commit was warranted.

Hypothesis: the remaining findings are stale incident custody plus `github_sync_publish_due`, not a live chain failure.

Recommendation: reconcile/close `inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e` against the completed m4/m5 milestones, then clear or publish the overdue GitHub-sync record for `problem-71f954a05546`.

decision.hypothesis:
ESCALATE

Pipeline friction map: `meta_repair` produced a no-output record, `repair-data` stayed pinned to the old m4 snapshot, watchdog now only reports the session as alive, and the auditor/problem ledger never reconciled that newer chain state or published the overdue GitHub-sync record.

Primary evidence still says operator action is due: the incident summary remains `outcome: "escalated"` with the blocking summary `meta_repair_running_stale` + `github_sync_publish_due` at [.megaplan/incident-ledger/summaries/incidents/inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/incident-ledger/summaries/incidents/inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.json:235). The linked problem is still open on the old m4 stall signature at [problem-71f954a05546.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/incident-ledger/summaries/problems/problem-71f954a05546.json:19).

That contradicts live chain corroboration: current chain state is already at `current_milestone_index: 11`, `current_plan_name: "m7a-output-format-route-20260705-0102"`, `last_state: "between_milestones"`, `pr_number: 89`, while `m4`, `m5`, `m6a`, and `m6b` are all recorded `done` in [chain-842114799446.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json:3) and [chain-842114799446.json](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/.chains/chain-842114799446.json:168). Watchdog independently observed the same session alive at m7a in [watchdog.log](/workspace/watchdog.log:39610) and [watchdog-report.json](/workspace/watchdog-report.json:105). The chain log is still advancing with fresh executor invalidations in [.megaplan/cloud-chain-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log](/workspace/reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e/reigh-app/.megaplan/cloud-chain-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log:38).

The meta-repair failure is real but historical: the meta record says “returned no output” at [fa112169-7b6f-4ac6-aac5-841086ddb4be.json](/workspace/.megaplan/cloud-sessions/repair-data/meta/fa112169-7b6f-4ac6-aac5-841086ddb4be.json:4), and the run log ended without a recordable response at [20260704T121718Z-...log](/workspace/.megaplan/meta-runs/20260704T121718Z-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e.log:7). No Arnold source fix was proven from current evidence.

Recommendation: reconcile/close `inc-megaplan-chain-reigh-extension-composition-spine-epic-12d49a3e` against the live m7a chain state, then clear or publish the overdue GitHub-sync record for `problem-71f954a05546`.
